question.text: Vikram took an education loan in April 2022 for his daughter's MBA program and started repaying it from October 2022. He pays Rs.2,80,000 as interest on the education loan in FY 2024-25. Under Section 80E (old regime), what deduction can he claim?

answer.text: Rs.2,00,000 (capped at housing loan interest limit)
Rs.2,80,000 (full interest amount, no upper limit)
Rs.0 (deduction period of 8 years has expired)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 80E allows deduction for interest paid on education loan with NO upper limit. The deduction is available for 8 consecutive assessment years starting from the assessment year in which repayment first begins. Vikram started repaying in FY 2022-23 (AY 2023-24). FY 2024-25 (AY 2025-26) is the 3rd year of the 8-year window. So, Rs.2,80,000 is fully deductible. **The Trap:** Option A confuses 80E with 80C (which has a Rs.1.5L cap and covers loan principal, not interest). Option B confuses it with Section 24 housing loan interest. Option D miscounts the 8-year period. Note: only interest is deductible under 80E, not principal. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A tests 80E as a unique deduction with no monetary ceiling — distinguishing it from most other deductions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 80E has no upper limit — it is one of the few uncapped deductions. Advise clients to claim the full interest even if it is substantial. The deduction is only for interest, not principal repayment.
